stem: A fintech stores 5 TB of transaction data in S3 as CSV. Training jobs read the same columns repeatedly. What is the MOST cost-effective format change to improve training I/O?

options.C: Store as Avro only in Kinesis

explanation: Parquet is columnar and compressed, reducing scan cost and SageMaker training time for tabular ML.

wrongWhy.A: JSON is row-oriented and verbose for analytics.
wrongWhy.C: Avro is valid but Parquet dominates S3 analytics ML.
wrongWhy.D: DynamoDB wrong for bulk training scans.

stem: A company needs low-latency feature retrieval at inference AND batch features for nightly retraining. Which TWO capabilities should they implement? (Select TWO)

options.A: SageMaker Feature Store online store for real-time inference lookups
options.B: Amazon Athena as the sole feature serving layer
options.C: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to replace feature storage
options.D: SageMaker Feature Store offline store for batch training pipelines
options.E: AWS Glue DataBrew only, with no feature persistence layer

explanation: Feature Store online + offline stores give consistent features for inference and batch retraining—the canonical exam pattern.

wrongWhy.B: Athena queries data but is not a feature serving layer.
wrongWhy.C: Firehose ingests streams, not feature management.
wrongWhy.E: DataBrew prepares data but not serve features at inference.

stem: A team must visually profile, clean, and transform data without writing Spark code. Which service should they choose?
explanation: DataBrew is the visual data preparation service.

wrongWhy.B: Batch runs jobs but no visual prep.
wrongWhy.C: EMR needs Spark coding.
wrongWhy.D: Lambda limited for large transforms.

stem: Before training a credit model, engineers must detect if one demographic has disproportionately fewer positive labels. Which metric/tool pair is appropriate?

options.B: Difference in Proportions of Labels (DPL) with SageMaker Clarify

explanation: DPL is a pre-training bias metric; Clarify detects label proportion bias.

wrongWhy.A: RMSE is regression metric.
wrongWhy.C: Model Monitor is post-deployment.
wrongWhy.D: Ground Truth labels data.

stem: Real-time clickstream events must be ingested for feature computation with custom consumers. Which service?
explanation: Streams allows custom real-time consumers; Firehose is fully managed delivery.

wrongWhy.A: Firehose not for custom consumer logic.
wrongWhy.C: DataSync is migration.
wrongWhy.D: SQS not primary stream ingest.

stem: A healthcare company must identify PHI in S3 buckets automatically. Which service?
explanation: Macie discovers sensitive data including PII/PHI patterns in S3.

wrongWhy.B: Rekognition is vision.
wrongWhy.C: WAF protects web apps.
wrongWhy.D: GuardDuty is threat detection.

stem: Order the steps to prepare data for SageMaker training from raw S3 CSV:
explanation: Ingest/validate → transform → split → optional Feature Store registration for reuse.

stem: A labeling project needs human annotators with workflow quality control for image bounding boxes. Best choice?
explanation: Ground Truth manages labeling workflows at scale.

wrongWhy.B: Polly is text-to-speech.
wrongWhy.C: Translate is language.
wrongWhy.D: CloudFormation is IaC.

stem: Class imbalance causes poor recall on minority class. Which approach is MOST appropriate?

options.B: Apply stratified sampling and consider oversampling/SMOTE-class techniques

explanation: Stratified splits and resampling address imbalance; accuracy alone misleads.

wrongWhy.A: Accuracy misleading.
wrongWhy.C: Removes critical class.
wrongWhy.D: Validation required.

stem: Training data must be mounted with POSIX semantics across multiple training instances. Which storage?
explanation: EFS provides shared POSIX file system for multi-node training.

wrongWhy.A: S3 object not POSIX mount.
wrongWhy.C: Glacier archival.
wrongWhy.D: Snowball migration device.

stem: Which service helps validate data quality rules in ML pipelines?
explanation: Validating rules on datasets in pipelines

wrongWhy.B: Macie finds sensitive data, not pipeline quality rules.
wrongWhy.C: CloudTrail audits API calls.
wrongWhy.D: Rekognition is computer vision.

stem: A team must combine batch data from Amazon RDS and Amazon S3 for ETL at scale. Best approach?

options.B: AWS Glue or Amazon EMR with Spark

wrongWhy.A: Athena queries but does not orchestrate large multi-source ETL.
wrongWhy.C: SNS is messaging.
wrongWhy.D: Lambda alone is poor for large batch ETL.

stem: ML model artifacts in S3 must use customer-managed encryption keys. What should you configure?

wrongWhy.A: SSE-S3 does not use customer-managed KMS CMK.
wrongWhy.B: Public buckets violate security best practices.
wrongWhy.D: Encryption required for compliance scenarios.

stem: Select TWO services suitable for streaming ETL into Amazon S3 (Select TWO).

options.C: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

explanation: Firehose delivers streams to S3; Managed Flink processes streams with stateful ETL.

wrongWhy.B: Glacier is archival.
wrongWhy.D: Route 53 is DNS.

stem: A fraud model flags many uncertain predictions. How reduce labeling cost while keeping quality?

options.D: Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) human review loops

wrongWhy.A: Throws away valuable edge cases.
wrongWhy.B: Supervised problems need labels.
wrongWhy.C: Monitoring does not solve labeling.

stem: Training loss flat but gradients vanishing:
No ML team; need image label detection API:
Version models and require approval before prod:
A team needs foundation-model text generation with minimal ML infrastructure operations. Select TWO.

options.A: Train GPT from scratch on EC2

explanation: Bedrock and JumpStart provide FMs without building training clusters from scratch.

wrongWhy.A: High ops and cost.
wrongWhy.D: Edge migration device.

stem: Compare new model safely without user impact:

options.C: Script mode with ECR inference container

explanation: Script mode with ECR inference container

stem: Document entity extraction without custom NLP:

options.D: Autopilot AutoML end-to-end; AMT tunes your estimator

explanation: Autopilot AutoML end-to-end; AMT tunes your estimator

stem: Native ML CI/CD DAG on SageMaker:
Gradual production traffic to new model:
Select TWO resources commonly used to build and store container images for SageMaker custom inference (Select TWO).
explanation: ECR stores container images; CodeBuild often builds and pushes images in CI/CD pipelines.

wrongWhy.C: Glacier not for container registry.
wrongWhy.D: DynamoDB is NoSQL.

stem: Private subnet inference no public internet:
Audit API calls for model deployment:
Alert when ML spend exceeds budget:
Select TWO approaches to reduce SageMaker training cost while maintaining recoverability (Select TWO).

options.A: Managed Spot Training with S3 checkpointing

explanation: Spot + checkpoints saves money with fault tolerance; rightsizing avoids overpayment.

wrongWhy.B: Oversized instances waste money.
wrongWhy.C: Logging needed for ops.
wrongWhy.D: Spot without checkpoints loses progress on interrupt.
